# Scenarios

- [I live in Brunswick - do I file a RITA 10A?](brunswick-resident-wrong-agency.md) - The wrong-agency trick question - Brunswick self-administers its income tax and is not a RITA member, so RITA forms do nothing for Brunswick-side taxes.
- [RITA suburb resident, Cleveland office - the two-agency case](cleveland-office-cca-boundary.md) - Cleveland is CCA, not RITA - refunds of Cleveland withholding follow CCA's process, while the residence-side consequences run through RITA. How to keep the two straight.
- [Under-18 with a summer job - the age exemption refund](under-18-summer-job.md) - From tax year 2024 every municipality exempts under-18 wages, so withheld tax comes back via Form 10A claim reason 1 with proof of birthdate - earlier years varied by city.
- [I worked from home in 2020-2021 - can I still claim?](wfh-2020-2021-why-you-cant-anymore.md) - The trick question - no. 2020 fails on the merits (Schaad) and on the deadline; 2021 was claimable but its window closed in April 2025. The history, briefly.
- [Flagship - Medina resident, Solon office, hybrid 2024](wfh-2024-medina-resident-solon-office.md) - The worked example - a hybrid worker withheld 2.0% to Solon all year claims WFH days on Form 10A; because Medina credits only 25%, the refund is mostly real money.
